summary: George Russell arrives at his home British Grand Prix brimming with confidence after executing a mature, calculated victory in Austria, positioning Mercedes to challenge Red Bull's dominance on a circuit that historically favors precision-engineered machinery. McLaren's recent pace improvements threaten to disrupt the established order, with Lando Norris and Oscar Piastri capable of exploiting any tactical vulnerability from the frontrunners over Silverstone's demanding high-speed corners. A Mercedes victory on home soil would slash Red Bull's championship lead and signal a genuine three-way fight for the title, reshaping the narrative of a season that appeared decidedly in Max Verstappen's favor.
